description: Al-Qaeda is a pan-Islamist militant organization founded in 1988 by Osama bin Laden and other veterans of the Soviet-Afghan War. It aims to unite the Muslim world under a caliphate and has conducted major attacks including the 1998 U.S. embassy bombings and the September 11 attacks. Designated a terrorist group by the UN and many countries, it operates globally with affiliates in various regions. Its current leader is Saif al-Adel.

country_links.evidence_quote: a barrage of cruise missiles launched by the U.S. devastated an al-Qaeda base in Khost, Afghanistan... a number of Taliban and al-Qaeda training camps were destroyed
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda had a base in Khost, Afghanistan, and operated training camps there.

country_links.evidence_quote: Al-Zawahiri's brother worked for the IIRO in Albania and had actively recruited on behalf of al-Qaeda.
country_links.context: Al-Zawahiri's brother worked for IIRO in Albania and recruited on behalf of al-Qaeda.

country_links.evidence_quote: Documents seized during the 2002 Bosnia raid showed that al-Qaeda widely exploited charities to channel financial and material support to its operatives across the globe.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda documents were seized in a raid in Sarajevo, Bosnia, showing exploitation of charities.

country_links.evidence_quote: Kashmiri was also charged by the US in a plot against Jyllands-Posten , the Danish newspaper which was at the center of the 2005 Muhammad cartoons controversy.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda plotted against the Danish newspaper Jyllands-Posten.

country_links.evidence_quote: Its membership is mostly composed of Egyptian Islamist leaders who participated in the anti-communist Afghan Jihad.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda's central leadership membership is mostly composed of Egyptian Islamist leaders.

country_links.evidence_quote: The drugs eventually are smuggled to Europe for distribution and sale, and the weapons are used in conflicts in Africa and possibly elsewhere.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda smuggled drugs to Europe for distribution and sale.

country_links.evidence_quote: Mohamed Atta, an Egyptian national studying in Germany at the time of his training, and other members of the Hamburg cell
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda had a cell in Hamburg, Germany, that included 9/11 hijackers.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2006, al-Qaeda claimed they had established a wing in Kashmir.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda established a wing in Kashmir and trained militants there.

country_links.evidence_quote: de facto leadership of al-Qaeda had passed to Saif Al-Adel, who was operating out of Iran.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda's de facto leader Saif Al-Adel was operating out of Iran.

country_links.evidence_quote: In Iraq, al-Qaeda forces loosely associated with the leadership were embedded in the Jama'at Al-Tawhid wal-Jihad group... In November 2010, the militant group Islamic State of Iraq, which is linked to al-Qaeda in Iraq, threatened to 'exterminate all Iraqi Christians'.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda forces were embedded in the insurgency in Iraq and threatened Iraqi Christians.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Israeli security and intelligence services believe al-Qaeda has managed to infiltrate operatives from the Occupied Territories into Israel, and is waiting for an opportunity to attack.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda infiltrated operatives from the Occupied Territories into Israel, waiting to attack.

country_links.evidence_quote: bin Laden instructed a Portuguese convert to Islam, Paulo Jose de Almeida Santos, to assassinate Zahir Shah... Santos entered the king's villa in Rome...
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda instructed a Portuguese convert to assassinate the former Afghan king in Rome.

country_links.evidence_quote: al-Qaeda planned attacks to coincide with the millennium... which would include the bombing of Christian holy sites in Jordan
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda planned to bomb Christian holy sites in Jordan.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 1998, al-Qaeda conducted the US embassy bombings in Kenya and Tanzania...
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda conducted the 1998 US embassy bombings in Kenya.

country_links.evidence_quote: Other sources of income included the heroin trade and donations from supporters in Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and other Gulf states.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda receives donations from supporters in Kuwait.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2011, al-Qaeda's North African wing condemned Libyan leader Muammar Gaddafi and declared support for the Anti-Gaddafi rebels... various Islamist militant groups affiliated with al-Qaeda were able to expand their operations in the region.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda's North African wing supported Anti-Gaddafi rebels and affiliated groups expanded operations in Libya.

country_links.evidence_quote: In Mali, the Ansar Dine faction was also reported as an ally of al-Qaeda in 2013.
country_links.context: Ansar Dine faction was reported as an ally of al-Qaeda in Mali.

country_links.evidence_quote: In November 2001, Kathmandu airport went on high alert after threats that bin Laden planned to hijack a plane and crash it into a target in New Delhi.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda threatened to hijack a plane from Kathmandu airport to crash into New Delhi.

country_links.evidence_quote: al-Qaeda was thought to have established bases in Pakistan administered Kashmir ... and continued to operate there with tacit approval of Pakistan's Intelligence services.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda established bases in Pakistan-administered Kashmir and operates there.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 1996, bin Laden personally engineered a plot to assassinate U.S. president Bill Clinton while the president was in Manila for the Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda plotted to assassinate U.S. President Bill Clinton in Manila.

country_links.evidence_quote: Several Qatari citizens have been accused of funding al-Qaeda.
country_links.context: Qatari citizens have been accused of funding al-Qaeda, and Qatar financed al-Qaeda's enterprises through Jabhat Al-Nusra.

country_links.evidence_quote: Other sources of income included the heroin trade and donations from supporters in Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and other Gulf states.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda receives donations from supporters in Saudi Arabia.

country_links.evidence_quote: Somali Islamist organization Al-Shabaab occasionally harboured al-Qaeda leaders in Somalia
country_links.context: Al-Shabaab harboured al-Qaeda leaders in Somalia.

country_links.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda was also possibly using aircraft to transport drugs and weapons from South America to various unstable countries in West Africa.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda used aircraft to transport drugs and weapons from South America to West Africa.

country_links.evidence_quote: From 1992, al-Qaeda established its headquarters in Sudan until it was expelled in 1996.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda established its headquarters in Sudan from 1992 until it was expelled in 1996.

country_links.evidence_quote: The number of al-Qaeda militants was set at 20,000 in Syria alone
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda had 20,000 militants in Syria.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 1998, al-Qaeda conducted the US embassy bombings in Kenya and Tanzania...
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda conducted the 1998 US embassy bombings in Tanzania.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2003, Islamists carried out a series of bombings in Istanbul... they specifically declined to pledge allegiance to al-Qaeda they asked for its blessing and help.
country_links.context: Islamists carried out bombings in Istanbul with al-Qaeda's blessing.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2009, three Londoners... were convicted of conspiring to detonate bombs disguised as soft drinks on seven airplanes bound for Canada and the U.S.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda-linked plotters in London conspired to bomb airplanes bound for Canada and the U.S.

country_links.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda's September 11 attacks (9/11) on America killed 2,996 people... the bombing of Los Angeles International Airport by Ahmed Ressam
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda carried out the September 11 attacks in the U.S. and planned to bomb Los Angeles International Airport.

country_links.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda was also possibly using aircraft to transport drugs and weapons from South America to various unstable countries in West Africa.
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda transported drugs and weapons to West Africa for distribution.

country_links.evidence_quote: On December 29, 1992, al-Qaeda launched the 1992 Yemen hotel bombings... On October 12, 2000, al-Qaeda militants in Yemen bombed the missile destroyer USS Cole in a suicide attack...
country_links.context: Al-Qaeda launched bombings in Yemen and its militants bombed the USS Cole there.
